info.title: Zadana API - APIDog Detailed Folders
info.description: OpenAPI document with detailed folder-style tags for APIDog import.

get.description: Returns favorite products for the authenticated customer, or for the guest device identified by X-Device-Id.

401.description: Unauthorized when neither Bearer token nor X-Device-Id is provided.

parameters.name: X-Device-Id
parameters.description: Optional for authenticated users. Required for guest favorites access and should be the same stable device id used by cart.

post.description: Adds a master product to favorites for the authenticated customer, or for the guest device identified by X-Device-Id.

401.description: Unauthorized when neither Bearer token nor X-Device-Id is provided.

parameters.name: X-Device-Id
parameters.description: Optional for authenticated users. Required for guest favorites access and should be the same stable device id used by cart.

delete.description: Removes all favorite products for the authenticated customer, or for the guest device identified by X-Device-Id.

401.description: Unauthorized when neither Bearer token nor X-Device-Id is provided.

parameters.name: X-Device-Id
parameters.description: Optional for authenticated users. Required for guest favorites access and should be the same stable device id used by cart.

delete.description: Removes one product from favorites for the authenticated customer, or for the guest device identified by X-Device-Id.

parameters.name: X-Device-Id
parameters.description: Optional for authenticated users. Required for guest favorites access and should be the same stable device id used by cart.

401.description: Unauthorized when neither Bearer token nor X-Device-Id is provided.

get.description: Returns active category-level items for the home categories section. In a hierarchy like activity > sub-activity > category > subcategory, this endpoint returns the category level.

get.summary: Get products for the selected category
get.description: Returns products for the selected category id only. Use a main category id to get its products, or use a subcategory id to get that subcategory's products. Typical flow: call GET /api/categories/{categoryId}/filters, read subcategories, then call GET /api/categories/{selectedSubcategoryId}/products. Filter query params are optional.

parameters.name: categoryId
parameters.description: Selected category or subcategory id whose products should be returned.

parameters.name: product_type_id
parameters.description: Optional product type filter. Use one of the ids returned by GET /api/categories/{categoryId}/filters.

parameters.name: part_id
parameters.description: Optional part filter. Use one of the ids returned by GET /api/categories/{categoryId}/filters.

parameters.name: quantity_id
parameters.description: Optional unit filter. Use one of the ids returned by GET /api/categories/{categoryId}/filters.

parameters.name: brand_id
parameters.description: Optional brand filter. Use one of the ids returned by GET /api/categories/{categoryId}/filters.

parameters.name: min_price
parameters.description: Optional minimum selling price filter.

parameters.name: max_price
parameters.description: Optional maximum selling price filter.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Optional sorting: newest, price_low_high, price_high_low, best_selling, highest_rated, alphabetical.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Optional page number. Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Optional page size. Defaults to 20.

get.summary: Get all products or filter by category
get.description: Returns all products when categoryId is not provided. If categoryId is provided as a query parameter, returns only products for that category. This is the recommended endpoint when the category selection may be empty.

parameters.name: categoryId
parameters.description: Optional category or subcategory id. Leave empty to return all products.

parameters.name: product_type_id
parameters.description: Optional product type filter. If categoryId is provided, use one of the ids returned by GET /api/categories/{categoryId}/filters.

parameters.name: part_id
parameters.description: Optional part filter. If categoryId is provided, use one of the ids returned by GET /api/categories/{categoryId}/filters.

parameters.name: quantity_id
parameters.description: Optional unit filter. If categoryId is provided, use one of the ids returned by GET /api/categories/{categoryId}/filters.

parameters.name: brand_id
parameters.description: Optional brand filter. If categoryId is provided, use one of the ids returned by GET /api/categories/{categoryId}/filters.

parameters.name: min_price
parameters.description: Optional minimum selling price filter.

parameters.name: max_price
parameters.description: Optional maximum selling price filter.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Optional sorting: newest, price_low_high, price_high_low, best_selling, highest_rated, alphabetical.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Optional page number. Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Optional page size. Defaults to 20.

get.description: Returns filter options for the selected category. This endpoint does not accept query params; use the returned ids and ranges with GET /api/categories/{categoryId}/products. If the user selects a subcategory from the response, send that subcategory id as the route categoryId in the next /products request.

parameters.name: categoryId
parameters.description: Selected category id used to build the filter sheet.

get.description: Returns all active leaf subcategories when called without categoryId. If categoryId is provided as a query parameter, returns only the active direct subcategories of that parent category.

parameters.name: categoryId
parameters.description: Optional parent category id. Leave it empty to return all active leaf subcategories, or send it to return that parent category's direct active subcategories.

get.summary: Get subcategories for a specific category
get.description: Returns the active direct subcategories for the provided parent category id.

parameters.name: categoryId
parameters.description: Parent category id whose direct active subcategories should be returned.

get.description: Returns active brands for the customer app brand listing.
Returns the selected active brand header data for the customer app.

parameters.name: brandId
parameters.description: Selected brand id.

get.description: Returns filter options for the selected brand. This endpoint does not accept query params; use the returned ids and ranges with GET /api/brands/{brandId}/products.

parameters.name: brandId
parameters.description: Selected brand id used to build the filter sheet.

get.summary: Get products for the selected brand
get.description: Returns products for the selected brand id only. Call GET /api/brands/{brandId}/filters first to load available filters, then send the selected ids and price range to this endpoint. Filter query params are optional.

parameters.name: brandId
parameters.description: Selected brand id whose products should be returned.

parameters.name: category_id
parameters.description: Optional category filter. Use one of the ids returned by GET /api/brands/{brandId}/filters.

parameters.name: subcategory_id
parameters.description: Optional subcategory filter. Use one of the ids returned by GET /api/brands/{brandId}/filters.

parameters.name: unit_id
parameters.description: Optional unit filter. Use one of the ids returned by GET /api/brands/{brandId}/filters.

parameters.name: min_price
parameters.description: Optional minimum selling price filter.

parameters.name: max_price
parameters.description: Optional maximum selling price filter.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Optional sorting: newest, price_low_high, price_high_low, best_selling, highest_rated, alphabetical.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Optional page number. Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Optional page size. Defaults to 20.

get.description: Returns customer product details, including master product id, default vendor product id, vendor price comparison, and similar products.

parameters.name: productId
parameters.description: Product id from customer flows. The backend resolves it and returns both master_product_id and default_vendor_product_id.

get.description: Searches customer products using a product-based result model. Each result row represents one product only, using one representative visible offer for card fields such as store, price, old_price, and image_url.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Search text entered by the user.

parameters.name: category_id
parameters.description: Optional category filter.

parameters.name: brand_id
parameters.description: Optional brand filter.

parameters.name: min_price
parameters.description: Optional minimum selling price filter.

parameters.name: max_price
parameters.description: Optional maximum selling price filter.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Optional sorting: newest, price_low_high, price_high_low, best_selling, highest_rated, alphabetical.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Optional page number. Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Optional page size. Defaults to 20.

get.description: Returns the cart items for either the authenticated customer or a guest device identified by the X-Device-Id header. When vendor_id is not sent, products are returned without prices and the financial summary fields remain null until the customer selects a vendor. When vendor_id is sent, each cart item returns the price/options for that vendor only and the summary returns subtotal before discount, discountAmount, and totalAmount after discount when all cart items are priced by that vendor.

parameters.name: vendor_id
parameters.description: Optional vendor identifier. When omitted, the cart returns products without prices and the financial summary fields are null. When provided, each cart item returns the price/options for this vendor only, and the summary totals are calculated only if every cart item has a valid offer for that vendor.

parameters.name: X-Device-Id
parameters.description: Optional for authenticated customers. Required for guest cart access and should contain a stable device/session identifier.

delete.description: Removes all items from the cart owned by the authenticated customer or by the guest device provided in X-Device-Id.

parameters.name: X-Device-Id
parameters.description: Optional for authenticated customers. Required for guest cart access and should contain a stable device/session identifier.

get.description: Returns all active vendors that currently have available products in the app. Use this list to let the customer pick a vendor before calling GET /api/cart with vendor_id.

parameters.name: X-Device-Id
parameters.description: Optional for authenticated customers. Required for guest cart access and should contain a stable device/session identifier.

post.description: Adds a product to the cart owned by the authenticated customer or by the guest device provided in X-Device-Id. The item is returned without prices and the financial summary fields remain null until the customer selects a vendor using GET /api/cart/vendors and then requests GET /api/cart with vendor_id. If the product already exists in cart, the quantity is increased instead of creating a duplicate row.

parameters.name: X-Device-Id
parameters.description: Optional for authenticated customers. Required for guest cart access and should contain a stable device/session identifier.

patch.description: Updates the quantity of a single cart item for the cart owned by the authenticated customer or by the guest device provided in X-Device-Id. When vendor_id is sent, the same response returns the updated vendor-specific prices plus subtotal before discount, discountAmount, and totalAmount after discount so the client does not need to call GET /api/cart again. When vendor_id is omitted, vendorPrices stays empty and the financial summary fields remain null.

parameters.name: X-Device-Id
parameters.description: Optional for authenticated customers. Required for guest cart access and should contain a stable device/session identifier.

parameters.name: vendor_id
parameters.description: Optional vendor identifier. When provided, the updated cart item response includes vendor-specific prices and recalculated subtotal, discountAmount, and totalAmount for that vendor.

delete.description: Removes one item from the cart owned by the authenticated customer or by the guest device provided in X-Device-Id.

parameters.name: X-Device-Id
parameters.description: Optional for authenticated customers. Required for guest cart access and should contain a stable device/session identifier.

get.description: Returns the checkout payload used by the mobile checkout screen, including cart items, selected address, delivery slot, payment methods, applied promo code, and recalculated totals.

post.description: Applies a coupon to the current customer checkout context and returns the applied promo information with the recalculated checkout totals.

delete.description: Removes the currently applied promo code from the checkout context and returns the refreshed financial summary.

post.description: Legacy compatibility endpoint for older clients. It accepts the historical checkout payload, infers the new checkout flow behind the scenes, and returns the same order and optional payment payload used by POST /api/orders.

Places an order from the current customer checkout state. Card payments create a Paymob session and return a payment object with iframe_url and provider_reference. Cash and bank transfer create local payments without Paymob.
Processes Paymob webhook notifications, validates HMAC when configured, resolves the linked local payment using merchant_order_id or provider reference, and updates payment/order status idempotently.

get.description: Returns paginated real customer accounts from the user app with operational metrics, favorites count, and realtime presence fields.
Returns a single customer profile with address, order summary, favorites count, recent orders, and realtime presence fields.

id.description: Master product identifier used across customer product cards and add-to-cart flows.
Master product identifier used across customer product cards and add-to-cart flows.
Master product identifier used across customer product cards and add-to-cart flows.
Master product identifier. This matches master_product_id and is the product id to use for add-to-cart from customer screens.
Master product identifier used across customer product cards and add-to-cart flows.

CartVendorPriceDto.description: Represents one available store offer for the cart product. Use the returned id as the vendor offer identifier when the user chooses a specific store/price later in the flow.

id.description: Vendor offer identifier for this product price option. This is the id the app should keep/send later when the user selects a specific store offer.

name.description: Localized store name shown to the customer.

price.description: Current selling price offered by this store.

old_price.description: Previous price for this store offer when discounted.

is_discounted.description: Indicates whether this store offer is currently discounted.

subtotal.description: Total product price before discount for the selected vendor. Null when vendor_id is not provided or when one or more cart items cannot be priced by that vendor.

discount_amount.description: Total savings for discounted cart items for the selected vendor. Null when the financial totals cannot be computed.

total_amount.description: Final product total after discount for the selected vendor. Null when vendor_id is not provided or when one or more cart items cannot be priced by that vendor.

id.description: Master product identifier used across customer product cards and add-to-cart flows.

Bearer.description: JWT Authorization header using the Bearer scheme. Example: "Authorization: Bearer {token}". Note: some customer cart endpoints can also be used as guest endpoints with X-Device-Id and therefore do not require Bearer.
